text:
Let the time it takes Tom to catch up be \( t \) hours.

Bob's head start: 75 miles  
Bob's speed: 55 mph  
Tom's speed: 70 mph  
Relative speed (Tom catching up): \( 70 - 55 = 15 \) mph

Time to catch up:  
\[
t = \frac{\text{distance}}{\text{relative speed}} = \frac{75}{15} = 5 \text{ hours}
\]

#### 5
